pattern: Tally how often each digit appears, then check that the digit at every position equals the tally of that position's index value.
hints: First build a frequency table: how many times does each digit 0..9 appear in the string?
The character at index i is the claimed count of the digit i. Compare the claim against the real frequency.
For every i, the frequency of the digit i must equal int(num[i]); if any position disagrees return false, otherwise true.

edge_inputs.description: single digit zero claims no zeros but has one
single digit one claims one zero but has none
count exceeds the length so it cannot hold
all same digit fails the self-count

stress.description: random digit strings up to length 10, mostly false with occasional true
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 10)
    num = ''.join(rng.choice('0123456789') for _ in range(n))
    return [num]
stress.note: short strings so brute and optimal both trivially terminate
